abstract: In both developing and developed nations, the pursuit of economic growth must increasingly be reconciled with the imperatives of sustainable development. Since the Industrial Revolution, rapid global transformation has often come at a significant environmental cost, characterized by the depletion of natural resources and a heavy reliance on fossil fuels. Despite this industrial progress, the resulting prosperity has not been equitably distributed, and traditional economic models have struggled to generate sufficient employment to match the growing labor supply. This study aims to investigate the impact of "green jobs" on the contemporary labor market. Emerging as a critical sector since the 1980s, these roles offer a dual solution: providing a roadmap for environmental sustainability while creating robust employment opportunities to meet the needs of an expanding workforce.
